description: A toy: the object made for play. 'wile' (play) closes on 'mo', echoing 'themo', the classifier of crafted things — the play-thing, readable at sight. What a toy is for is in its first syllable; what it is made of is in its last.
sound_symbolism: 'wi'-'le' is light as its purpose, and 'mo' gives it just enough object-weight to be picked up and put down.
grammatical_notes: Noun. A counted toy may take optional 'themo': 'ta themo wilemo' (one toy); 'ta wilemo' is also grammatical. 'lopia wilemo phelu.' (The child holds a toy.) The Velveteen Rabbit turns on the word's limit: 'thia mia wilemo ma nai. thia shewo nai.' (You are not my toy. You are real.)

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Carved, sewn, stuffed, handed down: the pre-industrial toy was made by someone the child knew, and mended by them too, which is half of how it became beloved.
